original: Assassination attempt on Olivier de Clisson, Constable of France, by Pierre de Craon fails
John Fabricius dedicates earliest sunspot publication
Battle of Lowestoft, off Suffolk, England: English fleet beats Dutch
Hungary declares itself independent under Ferenc Rákóczi II (aka Francis II Rákóczi)
England signs Treaty of Madrid
Spain underwrites Preliminairy of Paris
Austria, Great Britain, and Modena sign a secret military treaty
Rhode Island becomes first colony to prohibit importation of slaves
Leonard Norcross patents a submarine diving suit
Mission San Luis Rey de Francia founded in California
First Mormon missionaries to the British Isles leave Kirtland, Ohio
Anthony Faas, of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, patents the 1st US accordion, having made improvements to both the keyboard, and to enhance the sound (Patent No. 11,062)
Samuel Butler publishes 1st part of "Erewhon" in Christchurch, New Zealand
Second Battle of Winchester, Virginia
Skirmish at Berryville, Virginia
US House of Representatives passes the 14th Amendment (Civil Rights)
Oscar Dunn becomes first African American to be elected a Lieutenant Governor, of Louisiana
Hurricane kills 300 in Labrador
Congress of Berlin begins, determines the territories of the states in the Balkan peninsula following the Russo-Turkish War of 1877–78
The USS Jeannette, under the command of George Washington De Long, sinks in the Arctic circle following 21 months of drifting after becoming trapped in the ice
Fire destroys nearly 1,000 buildings in Vancouver, British Columbia
US Congress creates Department of Labor
Two feet of snow accumulates in Rawlins, Wyoming
Eagle Avenue in the Bronx is cut out and named
Émile Levassor wins the first automobile race in history, the Paris-Bordeaux-Paris, taking 48 hours and 48 minutes (1,178 km)
Yukon Territory of Canada established, Dawson chosen as capital
In China, Baron von Kettler, the German minister to China, beats two young Boxers with his walking stick; when word of this circulates, rioting and arson spread throughout Peking during the night
Prussian Upper house gives 350 million marks to Poland
Lowest temperature ever in 48 US states for June, 2°F in Tamarack, California
Pilot Charles Hamilton makes 1st 1-day round-trip from NY to Philadelphia
South Carolina physician William Demosthenes Crum is appointed the US minister to Liberia
NY GiantChristy Mathewsonwins his 300th game
Yanks win 13th game of year after losing 36 games
World War I: the deadliest German air raid on London during World War I is carried out by Gotha G bombers and results in 162 deaths, including 46 children with 432 injuries
Phillies and Cards tie 8-8 in 19 innings
Longest recorded attack of hiccups begins when Charles Osborne gets the hiccups and continues for 68 years; he dies 11 months after they stop
Bene Berak, Palestine, founded
Gaston Doumergue elected as the 1st protestant French President
Yanks win by forfeit over Tigers, their 3rd forfeit win
American William DeHart Hubbard sets men's long jump world record at 7.89m (25 ft 10 3⁄4 in) in Chicago, Illinois
Charles Jenkins demonstrates the transmission of synchronized pictures and sound (early television)
22 people killed by hailstones in Siatista, Greece
Great Britain and France sign peace treaty
Federal Home Owners Loan Corporation authorized
First sodium vapor lamps installed in the US in Schenectady, NY
Hammond scores his 100th hundred, 116 for Gloucs v Somerset
2nd Belgian government of Van Zealand forms
Great Cricket innings of 232 by Stan McCabe v England at Trent Bridge
Jews injured & property destroyed in Przemyal, Poland
Paris evacuates before German advance
1st V-2 rocket launch, Peenemunde, Germany; reached 1.3 km
Germany lands 4 saboteurs on Long Island
The United States opens its Office of War Information, with Elmer Davis as head
US Office of Strategic Services (OSS) formed
First German V-1 flying bomb (Fieseler Fi 103) attack on London
German counterattack on Villers-Bocage, Normandy
Heerjansdam soccer team forms
Orokoe peninsula Okinawa captured, with 6,000 dead
First transcontinental round-trip flight in one day, from California to Maryland
King Umberto II of Italy abdicates after 24 days in the wake of the country declaring itself a republic
First night game at Fenway Park (Red Sox 5, White Sox 3)
South African parliament accept "Groups Area Act"
UN arm forces reach Pyongyang Korea
Soviet fighters shoots down Swedish Air Force Dakota DC-3 reconnaissance plane east of Gotska Sandön in the Baltic Sea, kills entire crew of 8
KOAA TV channel 5 in Pueblo-Colorado Spgs, CO (NBC) 1st broadcast
Cornerstone of Albert Einstein College of Medicine laid in Bronx
Parliamentary election: Dutch Democrats 50/KVP 49
The last British troops leave the Suez Canal Zone in Egypt
Mayflower II from Plymouth, England, reaches Plymouth, Massachusetts
"Sammy Kaye Show" last airs on ABC-TV
"Alley-Oop" by Dyna-Sores peaks at #59
Prince Norodom Sihanoek becomes head of Cambodia
Norway named ombudsman
Basil Heatley runs world record marathon (2:13:55)
19th Tony Awards: "The Subject Was Roses" (play) & "Fiddler on the Roof" (musical) win
LPGA Western Open Women's Golf, Beverly CC: Susie Maxwell wins by 3 strokes ahead of runner-up Marlene Hagge
Vietnam War: Battle of Dong Xoai ends in a Viet Cong victory
US Supreme Court's Miranda decision; suspects must be informed of rights
"In The Summertime" by Mungo Jerry hits #1 in UK
Beatles' "Long and Winding Road" single goes #1 & stays #1 for 2 weeks
The Beatles' "Let It Be" album goes #1 and stays #1 for 4 weeks
"The New York Times" begins publishing excerpts from the Pentagon Papers, classified documents on the long history of the U.S. in Vietnam
In defiance of a government ban, members of the Orange Order march through the mainly Catholic town of Dungiven, County Londonderry, causing a riot

The Irish Republican Army invites British Secretary of State for Northern Ireland Willie Whitelaw to 'Free Derry'; Whitelaw rejects offer and reaffirms his policy to not "let part of the United Kingdom ... default from the rule of law"
Garvey, Lopes, Cey & Russell play together for 1st time, set record of staying together as an infield for 8½ years (LA Dodgers)
Henry Aaron addresses House of Representatives
IMF establishes its "oil facility", a special fund for loans to nations whose balance of payments have been severely affected by high oil prices
German sprinter Inge Helten runs female WR 100m (11.04) at Fürth, West Germany
Israeli Defense Forces withdraw from Lebanon
Sioux nation receives $100 million in compensation for Black Hills, South Dakota
US Congressman John Jenrette Jr (D-SC) indicted in "Abscam" investigation
39 Unification church couples wed in Germany
Fahd becomes King of Saudi Arabia after King Khalid dies at 69
NASA's Pioneer 10 becomes the 1st man-made object to leave the solar system
City of Berlin, Germany dedicates Léon Jessel Platz, a small town square, in memory of composer killed - at age 70 - by the Gestapo in 1943
Daniel Buettner, Bret Anderson, Martin Engel & Anne Knabe complete cycling journey of 15,266 mi from Prudhoe Bay, Alaska to Argentina
Boston Red Sox are 10 games back in AL, & go on to win AL East
Fed jury finds Liggett liable in death of NJ woman of lung cancer
US Supreme Court refuses to hear Yonkers argument they aren't racist
"Licence to Kill", 16th James Bond film, last directed by John Glen and starring Timothy Dalton premieres in London
Houston's Terry Puhl plays in his 1,403rd game (most by a Canadian)
Boeing 767-200ER sets a non-stop commercial flight record, flying 9,253 miles nonstop from Seattle to Nairobi, Kenya
Washington, D.C. mayor Marion Barry announces he will not seek a 4th term
NHL owners present contract to players (leads to Apr 1, 1992 strike)
The National, the first all-sports daily newspaper, ceases publication
Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani re-elected President of Iran
A jury in Anchorage, Alaska, blames recklessness by Exxon and Captain Joseph Hazelwood for the Exxon Valdez disaster, allowing victims of the oil spill to seek $15 billion in damages
New Jersey Devils beat Philadelphia Flyers, 4-2 for a 4-2 series win; qualify for their first Stanley Cup Finals Series (beat Detroit Red Wings, 4-0)
Montana Freemen surrender after an 81-day standoff with FBI agents
American fugitive Ira Einhorn is arrested in France for the murder of Holly Maddux after 16 years on the run, though he would not return for another four years
The United States of America withdraws from the Anti-Ballistic Missile Treaty
The US Senate issues a formal resolution apologizing for failure to create anti-lynching legislation
The Al Askari Mosque in Samarra is bombed reportedly by Iraqi Baathists, destroying the mosque's two ten-story minarets
64th Tony Awards: "Memphis" & "Red" (play) win
Christchurch, New Zealand is hit by another strong earthquake measuring magnitude 6.3
A series of bombings across Iraq kill 93 and wound 300 people
Stanley Cup Final, Staples Center, Los Angeles, CA: LA Kings edge New York Rangers, 3-2 in double OT for a 4-1 series win; second Championship in LA franchise history
Floods in Tbilisi, Georgia, kill 12 people and free animals from the city's zoo, including bears and hippos, to roam the city
Philae, 1st spacecraft to land on a comet in European Space Agency’s Rosetta mission re-awakes after 7 months
Some Democratic Party members of US House of Representatives protest 'moment of silence' as an inadequate response to mass shooting, and demand legislative action on gun control
Otto Warmbier returns to the US in an unresponsive state after being held in a North Korean jail for 17 months
Warriors win 2nd NBA title in 3 years over the Cavaliers (129-120)
Antarctica is melting at an accelerating rate - 200 billion tonnes a year, 3 trillion tonnes in 25 years, in report published in "Nature" journal
FIFA Congress votes to award 2026 World Cup to joint bid by US, Canada & Mexico
Raccoon climbs 23 story office building in St Paul, Minnesota, becoming an internet sensation
The Spanish Football Association sacks coach Julen Lopetegui just 2 days out from first World Cup game after he agreed to manage Real Madrid
Volkswagen fined €1 billion (£880m) by German prosecutors over diesel emissions scandal
Archaeologists announce Scottish crannogs (fortified settlements on artificial islands in lochs) far older than first thought, radiocarbon dated to 3640-3360 BC, older than Stonehenge
Kenyan MP Rashid Kassim arrested for slapping female MP Fatuma Gedi, following a walk out of parliament by female MPs after claiming male MPs joked "its slapping day"
G7 summits ends in Cornwall, England, with leaders promising one billion vaccine doses for poorer nations
US President Joe Biden has tea withQueen Elizabeth IIat Windsor Castle
A Google engineer claims one of its AI systems, Lamda, might have a sentient mind, causing the company to place him on leave
MIT researchers propose creating raft of "space bubbles" floating above the earth to reflect the sun's rays and help fight climate change
New York Stock Exchange enters bear market territory (defined as falling 20% from a recent high - Jan 3) after the S&P falls 3.9%, amid fears of high inflation and a recession
World's largest freshwater fish, a stingray 3.98m x 2.2m caught in the Mekong river, Cambodia (tagged and released)
Former US PresidentDonald Trumpand his aide Walt Nauta plead not guilty to federal charges of intentionally holding on to classified documents at a court in Miami

Stanley Cup Final, T-Mobile Arena, Las Vegas, NV: Vegas Golden Knights rout Florida Panthers 9-3 to clinch 4-1 series win; franchise's first title in only 6th year in the NHL; MVP: Jonathan Marchessault (VGK forward)
Argentina’s upper house narrowly passes President Javier Milei’s controversial economic reforms amid violent protests
G7 leaders agree to loan Ukraine $50 billion from frozen Russian assets to buy weapons and rebuild, at their meeting in Borgo Egnazia, Italy

Operation Rising Lion: Israel strikes dozens of targets in Iran, including nuclear facilities, military sites and private residences, killing some senior military commanders and scientists in effort to eliminate Iran’s nuclear ambitions
Peace of Adrianople (Treaty of Edirne) King Ferdinand of Austria agrees to pay Ottoman SultanSuleiman the Magnificent30,000 gold Forints for western Hungary
Marquis de Lafayettelands in the US
Mrs Alexander Hamilton serves ice cream for dessert toGeorge Washington
KingLouis XVIdismisses French government
Simon Bolívar proclaimed dictator of Colombia
Giuseppe Verdi's opera "Les vêpres sicilenne" (The Sicilian Vespers) premieres at the Paris Opéra
PresidentAndrew Johnsonproclaims reconstruction of confederate states
New York Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherChristy Mathewsonthrows his 2nd no-hitter, beating Chicago Cubs, 1-0 at West Side Grounds, Chicago
Canadian championTommy BurnsKOs Bill Squires of Australia in 8th round at Neuilly Bowling Palace, Paris to retain world heavyweight boxing title

Eleftherios Venizelosannounces that Greece intends to annex Chios and Mytilene, two large islands off the Turkish coast; Greece and Turkey have been heading towards conflict for months over claims to Aegean islands
Ticker-tape parade welcomes aviatorCharles Lindberghto NYC
German Secret State Police (Gestapo - Geheime Staats Polizei) established byHermann Goering
10-to-1 underdogJames J. Braddockupsets defending championMax Baerin a 15 round unanimous points decision at Madison Square Garden, NYC for the world heavyweight boxing title
Joe DiMaggiohits 3 consecutive HRs against St Louis Browns
Babe Ruth's final farewell at Yankee Stadium, he dies Aug 16th
Jim Petersruns world record marathon (2:18:40.2)
Ted Williamsbecomes 1st ALer to have 2, 3-HR games in a season
Frank Zappagraduates from Antelope Valley High School in Lancaster, California
Film "Lolita" is released, based onVladimir Nabokov's novel, directed byStanley Kubrickand starring James Mason and Sue Lyon
Thurgood Marshallnominated as 1st African American Supreme Court justice
Guitarist Mick Taylor leaves John Mayall Band and joinsThe Rolling Stones
Bob Marleyperforms at Jaap Edenhal, Amsterdam, Netherlands
Convicted Martin Luther King assassinJames Earl Rayrecaptured
Asylum Records releases "Mingus", Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchell's 10th studio album
Deborah Harry andMeat Loaffilm "Roadie" premieres
Paul McCartneyreleases "Waterfall"
UN Security Council calls for South Africa to freeNelson Mandela
Teenager fires 6 blank rounds atQueen Elizabeth II
Tom Snyderinterviews murdererCharles Mansonon "Tomorrow"
"Prizzi's Honor", starringJack Nicholson, Kathleen Turner, andAnjelica Huston, is released
USPresident Reagancriticizes South African state of emergency
George Harrisonreleases single "This Is Love"
Piano playing rock singerJerry Lee Lewisgets a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
Film "Dick Tracy", directed byWarren Beattyand starring Warren Beatty and Madonna, premieres in Woodstock, Illinois
Nelson andWinnie Mandelawelcomed in NYC
Chicago Cub 2nd basemanRyne Sandberg, retires due to poor play, he forfeits $15.7 million of his $25 million contract
Don Mattinglyplays 1st base 1,469 game for Yankees (2nd most)
"Jagged Little Pill", 3rd studio album byAlanis Morissetteis released (Grammy Award Album of the Year, 1996)
Björkreleases her second art pop solo album "Post"
MLB Cleveland Indians'Dennis Martínezno-hits Baltimore Orioles 11-0,
Jurors in Oklahoma City bombing trial sentenceTimothy McVeighto death
Italy pardons Mehmet Ali Agca, the Turkish gunman who tried to kill PopeJohn Paul IIin 1981
Samuel L. Jacksonis honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
South Korean PresidentKim Dae-jungmeets North Korean dictatorKim Jong-ilfor the beginning of the first-ever inter-Korean summit in the northern capital of Pyongyang
TNN/CMT Country Weekly Music Awards:George Strait,Faith Hill, andAlan Jacksonwin
Stanley Cup Final, Joe Louis Arena, Detroit, MI: Detroit Red Wings beat Carolina Hurricanes, 3-1 for a 4-1 series win; Red Wings' 10th title; coachScotty Bowmanretires with record 9th title
A jury in Santa Maria, California acquits pop singerMichael Jacksonof molesting 13-year-old Gavin Arvizo at his Neverland Ranch
First large-scale exhibition of Mexican artistFrida Kahlo's work in Mexico opens at Palacio de Bellas Artes on the 100th anniversary of her birth
"Viva la Vida" single released byColdplay, - their 1st US No. 1, (Grammy Song of the Year)
San Francisco Giants' Matt Cain pitches first perfect game in the franchise's history against the Houston Astros
US Attorney GeneralJeff Sessionstestifies before Senate Intelligence Committee, denies secretly meeting Russians
PresidentDonald Trumpannounces Sarah Sanders will be leaving her position as White House Press Secretary
Recently deposed Sudan PresidentOmar al-Bashiris charged with corruption in Sudan
US Secretary of StateMike Pompeosays Iran is responsible for recent attacks on oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman
Israel's Knesset votes in a new coalition government with Naftali Bennett as Prime Minister, oustingBenjamin Netanyahuafter a record 12 years
The Rolling Stones cancel concert in Amsterdam due to singerMick Jaggertesting positive for COVID-19
Elon Musksecures Tesla shareholders approval for a $46 billion pay deal - the largest in American corporate history
Anglo-Portuguese Treaty of Perpetual Alliance (world's oldest still in existence) is signed in London between KingEdward IIIof England and King Ferdinand I of Portugal
Peasants' Revolt: Rebels enter London and with local townsfolk, attack the prisons, destroy Savoy Palace and kill anyone associated with the royal government
US Post Office states that children cannot be sent by parcel post after various instances
